reasoning: The US has rapidly escalated its military presence near Venezuela, with the USS Gerald R. Ford arriving mid-November and Marines deployed extremely close to the coast. The US has already conducted 20+ strikes on boats in international waters (80 deaths). The trigger for YES under the rules would require an airstrike on Venezuelan soil, military-on-military conflict causing 10 deaths, or a boat strike within internationally recognized Venezuelan waters causing 10 deaths. Trump has been briefed on land strike options and stated he "sort of made up his mind" in mid-November. Given the 185 Tomahawks in theater and ongoing "Operation Southern Spear," the chance of an airstrike on land or an incursion into territorial waters before year-end is slightly better than a coin toss. The market was 46% on Nov 13, and the official launch of the operation plus the carrier's arrival since then slightly elevates the risk.
